question_text: A block of wood has a mass of 240 g and a volume of 400 cm³. A block of iron has a mass of 780 g and a volume of 100 cm³. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The wood is denser because it has a larger volume

options.label: B
options.text: The iron is denser because it has a smaller volume for a larger mass

options.label: C
options.text: They have the same density because both are solids

options.label: D
options.text: The wood is denser because it has a larger mass

explanation: To compare densities, we need to calculate density = mass ÷ volume. Wood: 240 ÷ 400 = 0.6 g/cm³. Iron: 780 ÷ 100 = 7.8 g/cm³. Iron is much denser because it packs more mass into a smaller space.
